title: Fortrade Expands Its Instrument Range With New Shares Across AI, Networking, Space, and Advanced Materials
author: Na
description: Fortrade has expanded its trading platform by adding shares in four companies spanning AI, optical networking, space systems, and advanced materials sectors. The new additions include Ciena Corporation, Rocket Lab Corporation, Corning Incorporated, and Cerebras Systems, available as CFDs. The expansion reflects growing client demand for access to companies driving technological innovation beyond mega-cap names.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a newly added trading instrument on Fortrade's platform, highlighting its relevance in optical networking and automation software sectors that are attracting trader interest.
Added to Fortrade's platform as a space company providing launch services and spacecraft manufacturing, reflecting market interest in space systems as a structural growth theme.
Included in the new instrument range for its specialty glass, optical communications, and display technologies, positioning it within the broader technology and advanced materials expansion.
Highlighted as a newly listed AI company developing large-scale processors for AI model training, representing the AI infrastructure theme driving current market interest.

title: Corning vs. QuantumScape: Which Technology Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Pamela Kock
description: Corning, a profitable materials-science leader with $15.6B in FY2025 revenue and 10.2% net margins, is compared against QuantumScape, a pre-revenue development-stage company pursuing solid-state battery technology. The article presents a classic risk-reward choice: Corning offers stability and proven profitability, while QuantumScape offers speculative upside potential but faces significant technical and production hurdles. The author recommends Corning for most investors due to its 175-year track record of adapting to technological change.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Established profitable company with strong FY2025 revenue growth (~19%), solid net margins (10.2%), healthy balance sheet (1.6x current ratio, 0.9x debt-to-equity), and demonstrated ability to adapt to technological change over 175 years. Author recommends it as the better choice for 2026.

Pre-revenue development-stage company with significant upside potential in solid-state battery technology but facing major technical and production-scale hurdles. High cash position (15.9x current ratio) provides runway, but company is burning capital with $435.1M net loss in FY2025. Represents speculative bet suitable only for high-risk-tolerance investors.
Mentioned as an established lithium-ion battery manufacturer with economies of scale that QuantumScape must compete against. No specific sentiment expressed about Tesla itself.
Mentioned as an established lithium-ion battery manufacturer that QuantumScape must compete against. No specific sentiment expressed.
Mentioned as a global competitor creating pricing pressure on Corning. No specific sentiment expressed.

title: Amazon Is the Latest AI Giant to Sign a Blockbuster Deal With This Under-the-Radar Semiconductor Company (Behind Nvidia and Meta)
author: Anthony Di Pizio
description: Corning has signed a multiyear deal worth approximately $6 billion with Amazon to supply optical connectivity solutions for data centers, joining similar commitments from Meta and Nvidia. The company's Multicore Fiber technology reduces cable requirements by 75% while improving performance. However, analysts caution that Corning's stock trades at a premium valuation (P/E of 61.7) relative to growth expectations, and investors should be prepared for a 5+ year holding period given execution risks and potential AI demand fluctuations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Major blockbuster deals with Amazon, Meta, and Nvidia; strong revenue growth (36% YoY in optical communications); expanding order pipeline of $12+ billion; improved profit margins due to high demand. However, positive sentiment is tempered by valuation concerns.

Signed major optical connectivity supply deal indicating investment in AI infrastructure, but the deal itself is not material enough to significantly impact Amazon's overall business or stock outlook.
Previously signed similar $6 billion optical connectivity deal with Corning, demonstrating commitment to AI infrastructure, but this is not new news and impact is already reflected in market.

Signed deal to help Corning expand manufacturing capacity, indicating strong demand for optical solutions in AI data centers, but this is supportive rather than directly impactful to Nvidia's business.
Mentioned only as a historical customer of Corning's glass products for iPhones; not relevant to the current AI infrastructure narrative.

title: Where Will Amazon Stock Be in 3 Years?
author: Daniel Sparks
description: Amazon's stock has nearly doubled over three years, but future performance hinges on AWS cloud growth and AI investment payoff. While AWS revenue accelerated 28% YoY and AI-related revenue runs above $15B annually, Amazon's planned $200B capex spending in 2026 strains free cash flow. With a P/E ratio in the mid-30s, the premium valuation leaves little room for error, suggesting conservative 10-12% annual returns over the next three years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While AWS shows strong growth (28% YoY) and AI revenue is accelerating ($15B+ annual rate), the massive $200B capex spending in 2026 significantly strains free cash flow (down to $1.2B from $25.9B). The premium P/E valuation (mid-30s) leaves limited upside, with analyst projecting only 10-12% annual returns over three years—lower than the past three years' performance.
Corning secured a multibillion-dollar deal with Amazon to supply fiber for U.S. data centers, benefiting from Amazon's massive AI infrastructure build-out and ongoing capex expansion.

title: Forget the Big Chipmakers for a Minute: This Overlooked AI Stock Is Up 114% and Just Landed Deals With Nvidia and Amazon
author: Daniel Sparks
description: Corning, a 175-year-old glassmaker, has emerged as a quiet beneficiary of AI infrastructure spending, with its stock up 114% in 2026. The company secured major multibillion-dollar deals with Amazon, Meta, and Nvidia for optical fiber connectivity in data centers. Corning's optical communications sales grew 36% year-over-year in Q1, and the company targets $40 billion in annualized sales by 2030. However, the stock trades at a rich P/E ratio of 90, and investors should be cautious about concentration risk tied to hyperscaler AI spending.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong revenue growth (36% YoY in optical communications), major deals with tech giants, expanding manufacturing capacity, and ambitious 2030 sales targets demonstrate solid business momentum in the AI build-out.

Mentioned as a customer securing multibillion-dollar fiber agreements with Corning for data center expansion, indicating continued AI infrastructure investment, but no direct impact on Amazon's core business highlighted.

Named Corning as optical partner for next-generation AI infrastructure with $500 million investment and potential $3.2 billion total commitment, demonstrating strategic commitment to AI ecosystem development.
Agreed to purchase up to $6 billion in optical solutions from Corning for AI data centers, showing continued infrastructure investment but no direct business impact highlighted.
